4. Salvetti/BRAID.md's headline object G K := ⨆ ConcGrpd(K^⊗n) ⋊ Sₙ does not exist in the code (no ⋊, no K^⊗n, no BrMonCat anywhere). STRUCTURAL FIX: tracked files pointed into ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/, a machine-local dir that is NOT in git — including a LEAN SOURCE FILE (Chains/Segal.lean:817, 'See [[cubechains-segal]]'). Anyone cloning the repo got dangling pointers into one laptop. All removed.

DIAGNOSIS of why this happened (the real deliverable): the junk prose is DEFENSIVE, not explanatory — 'sorry-free' x10, 'unconditional' x7 in one file, 'assumption-free' x7 in another. All true, all worthless to a reader, all addressed to the requester. It is a status report to a grader, misfiled into a source file. Prior instruction attempts failed because they were PROHIBITIONS WITHOUT DESTINATIONS: 'don't write process prose' lost to 'leave the next agent enough context', which had a concrete affordance (a markdown file) while the prohibition had none. Beads supplies the missing destination.

THE RULE, now in CLAUDE.md: if a fact would become false when a bead closes, it belongs in the bead, not in a file. Plus three MECHANICAL tests (would it become false on close? does it have a tense? does it read as an argument that your work is good?), budgets not adjectives (<=1 line of intent per declaration, <=10 per module docstring), and a destinations table. Key correction discovered mid-work: bd remember is NOT git-portable (issues.jsonl exports only _type:issue; the Dolt DB is gitignored; a clone fetches refs/heads/* only) — so durable knowledge that must survive a clone goes in a PINNED BEAD or in git, and CLAUDE.md stays the constitution because it is the only thing that loads when bd is absent.

Remaining work is tracked under Cubical-ndf. Open: w7x (BRAID.md, needs the user's math call).

AUDIT DONE. Two headline findings.

1. UNCONDITIONALITY: CONFIRMED, by Lean rather than by reading imports.
   hasGlobalEventNaming_iff_braidPure : ∀ (K : BPSet), HasGlobalEventNaming K ↔ BraidPure K
   axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]. Same for braidFunctor.
   Landmine #1 (equivWedgeCat silently carrying NSL/AdmitsAltitude) CANNOT be present: a
   hypothesis-free statement that compiles sorry-free with no extra axioms has nowhere to hide a
   side condition. Importing a file that merely CONTAINS conditional theorems is harmless. The
   import cone is therefore irrelevant to unconditionality — it only matters for the file surgery.

2. THE §8.1 CLAIM IS WRONG. Cone = 58 of 124 files: Salvetti 18, Chains 12, SCHEDULE 11,
   Arrangements 10, Foundations 7. §8.1 says the braid result needs THREE Schedule files
   (EventNaming, EventMapBij, Orientation). It actually pulls in ELEVEN — including Space, Cover,
   Cone, ChainCone, Atlas, HDA, EventLocalSystem, and LabelSpace. The 'schedule space, atlas, cones,
   charts' that §8.1 tells you to quarantine are INSIDE the cone. Only 5 of 16 Schedule files
   (COMSheaf, Horizon, LabelChart, LocalCOM, Sculpture) are quarantinable as things stand.

BUT all 11 enter through exactly THREE ACCIDENTAL EDGES, each thin:
  a) Salvetti/BraidFunctor -> Schedule/Orientation, for TWO names: orSign, eventEquiv_id.
     This single import drags in Cover -> LabelSpace and the whole schedule-space stack.
     orSign/eventEquiv_id are EVENT-layer facts; they sit in Orientation.lean only because that is
     where w₁ happened to be developed.
  b) Salvetti/Normalize -> Schedule/Atlas, for SEVEN names: bslice, pbead, psz, psz_pos, pcubes,
     pchain, prefine. Verified: Atlas lines ~640-940 are pure CHAIN plumbing with zero references to
     Sched/Chart/Stratum/schedCone. They are misfiled — they belong in Chains/.
  c) Schedule/EventMapBij -> Schedule/Cone, for schedCone + 3 friends, used ONLY in the trailing
     corollaries (lines 375-386), AFTER eventMap_bijective (361) and eventMap_surjective (368). The
     bijectivity proof itself does not touch Cone.

ROOT CAUSE, and the thing §8.1 missed: eventEquiv is DEFINED IN Schedule/Space.lean:52 —
  eventEquiv f := Equiv.ofBijective (eventMap f) (eventMap_bijective f)
An event-layer definition parked inside the schedule-SPACE module. That is why Space (and hence the
atlas/cone/chart stack) is in the cone at all. §5 is right that eventMap_bijective is load-bearing;
it is just in the wrong folder.

THE FIX — an Events/ layer, which makes ALL of Schedule/ quarantinable (11 -> 0):

Then Salvetti/ imports only Events/, and the schedule space, atlas, cones, charts, LabelSpace,
Orientation's w₁, COMSheaf, Horizon, Sculpture, LabelChart, LocalCOM all quarantine cleanly.

ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED AND FIXED: eventEquiv was defined in Schedule/Space.lean:52 as
Equiv.ofBijective (eventMap f) (eventMap_bijective f) — an event-layer definition parked inside the
schedule-SPACE module. That one misfiling is why Space/Atlas/Cone/LabelSpace were in the braid cone.

THREE THINGS THE PLAN DID NOT ANTICIPATE (all forced, all resolved):
1. Normalize also needed the Fintype (EventObj a) INSTANCE, which it was getting through Atlas's import
   chain — invisible to a name-based analysis. Moved to EventNaming next to EventObj.
2. eventEquiv_comp lived in Schedule/Cover.lean:86, and orSign_comp needs it. It went to Events/EventMapBij
   with eventEquiv (putting it in OrdSign would have forced Cover to import OrdSign).
3. Orientable + intUnits_inv_self are used by Salvetti/BraidCharacters (evPerm_sign_coboundary_of_orientable).
   So Orientable had to move to Events/OrdSign; leaving it behind would have kept the edge alive.
   Schedule/Orientation keeps exactly orChar + orientable_of_hasGlobalEventNaming + orientable_of_labelling
   — the w1 story — and its Schedule/Cover import turned out to be DEAD and was dropped.

REMAINING EDGE: Salvetti/SalLocal.lean -> Schedule/LocalCOM. SalLocal is OFF the braid cone (PurityHDA
never reaches it), so it quarantines WITH Schedule/ rather than blocking it. See the note on Cubical-217.

DECISION: OPTION B — repoint is the idiom. Endpoints are a PARAMETER, not a commitment.

The evidence is decisive and it was already sitting in the repo:
- BPSet = <toPsh, init, final>. repoint K u v swaps only the two vertices; toPsh is untouched, so
  (K.repoint u v).cells n = K.cells n by rfl, and K.repoint K.init K.final = K by rfl (structure eta).
- Flow/ ALREADY runs entirely on this idiom: flowHom K u v := ConcGrpd (K.repoint u v), plus flowId,
  flowComp, chConcMor_assoc and the unit laws — all proved against Ch (K.repoint u v).

Option A (generalize Ch/Lines/ConcCat to take endpoints) would rebuild by hand exactly what repoint
gives for free, and would break every one of those existing lemmas. It is pure churn. Rejected.

NOTE the §8.2 premise ('BPSet is bipointed and most of the development does not need it') is
MISLEADING. The bipointing is STRUCTURAL, not spurious: Ch K is by definition the chains from K.init
to K.final, so Ch — and everything downstream (Lines, ConcCat, braidFunctor) — genuinely needs a
BPSet. What those files do NOT do is READ the endpoints: BraidFunctor, Lines, ConcGroupoid and
EventNaming contain ZERO references to K.init/K.final. They need endpoints to EXIST, not to be
anything in particular. Parameterizing them (repoint) is therefore the whole and correct answer.

DONE. lake build CubeChains green (2012 jobs).

repoint was misfiled: a 20-line pure-BPSet operation living in Flow/ChainConcat.lean, at the TOP of the stack (it imports Chains.Segal). So nothing below Flow/ could say Ch (K.repoint u v) without pulling in the whole chain layer — the same class of misfiling as eventEquiv in Schedule/Space.lean.

Moved repoint + repoint_toPsh/_init/_final/_self into Foundations/Bipointed.lean, next to the BPSet structure it operates on, and added repoint_repoint (rfl). It is now reachable from every layer, so Fund/CFund can be stated with hom(u,v) = Ch (K.repoint u v) without depending on Flow/.

This unblocks enr-fund (Cubical-j28) and enr-cfund (Cubical-1mg).

NB: dropped the bogus 'blocked by iso-root' edge. I added it when seeding the board on the theory that refactors should land after the isolation, but a 20-line relocation does not need a smaller surface. The graph was wrong, not the work.

NO CHANGE NEEDED — a finding, not a punt.

§8.2 says 'delete the bipointing from everything that does not use it'. There is nothing to delete:
1. The bipointing is STRUCTURAL. Ch K is the chains from K.init to K.final. You cannot remove BPSet from Ch / Lines / ConcCat — it is what makes a chain a chain.
2. The braid stack never READS the endpoints: BraidFunctor.lean, Lines.lean, ConcGroupoid.lean, Events/EventNaming.lean have ZERO references to K.init/K.final. They need endpoints to EXIST, not to be anything particular. repoint parameterizes exactly that, and it now lives in Foundations.
3. The presheaf-level / BPSet-level split ALREADY EXISTS where it should: Chains/WedgeMap has 6 presheaf-level glue0_* cores under 9 BPSet wedge2_* wrappers; Foundations/Altitude states NonSelfLinked/IsAltitude at PrecubicalSet level and wraps them for BPSet.

The de-bipointing worth doing was Cubical-2g7 (make endpoints a parameter, low in the stack). It is done. Stripping further would be inventing churn.

All three children resolved. Decision: repoint is the idiom (endpoints are a parameter, not a commitment). Execution: repoint moved from Flow/ChainConcat to Foundations/Bipointed, reachable from every layer. Stripping: nothing to strip — the bipointing is structural, and the presheaf/BPSet split already exists where it belongs.

The epic's own title ('most of the development does not need it') turned out to be wrong, and that is the useful output: the development DOES need bipointing (Ch K is by definition chains init->final); what it does not need is the endpoints to be FIXED. That distinction is the whole ticket.
Built and building clean; axiom-clean (propext/choice/Quot.sound only). Braid n, permLen, germRels, ofPerm, ofPerm_mul, ofPerm_one, permHom, permHom_surjective, PureBraid.

Braid/{BlockPerm,Jux,Category}.lean all build clean. blockPerm extracted from Salvetti/BraidDeloop (no proof changes); permLen_blockPerm proved; juxHom via MonoidHom.noncommCoprod; Braids = Sigma n, SingleObj (Braid n) with Groupoid instance; braidsTensor + braidsTensor_map (composition |-> juxtaposition).

Braid/Functor.lean builds, axiom-clean. invCount_eq_permLen (bridge), permLen_evPerm_comp (THE germ relation, derived from writhe being a functor + writhe_braidPsi), braidPsiGerm : ConcCatN K n => BraidFib n (f |-> ofPerm (evPerm f)), braidPhi := FreeGroupoid.lift, permHom_braidPhi (Phi lifts the event monodromy). Per-stratum; global grading split out.

chambersConcat assoc + BOTH units proved, first try, axiom-clean. Key move: state it INDEX-WISE (chambersConcat_assoc_val), where the recursion on A goes through with no HEq fighting; heq_of_chambers then substs the list equality away. Left unit is definitional (rfl). The predicted landmine #2 (erw against serialWedge type args) never fired. (Forced: the blocking bead 9e5 is decided -- Option A -- but is a user-closes bead.)
Closed

braidGrading : ConcCat K ⥤ Braids and braidGrpd : ConcGrpd K ⥤ Braids landed in Braid/Grading.lean, sorry-free. obj x = strands (nEvents x); map f = braidHom (ofPerm (evPerm' f)) ≫ eqToHom recast. Verified by rfl.
CFund K built: def CFund + EnrichedCategory Cat (CFund K) in Flow/CFund.lean, sorry-free. Bicategory.Strict (CatEnriched (CFund K)) derives by inferInstance.
lift₂ / lift₂_spec / lift₂_ext / lift₃_ext / natTrans₂_ext all landed in Foundations/FreeGroupoidLift.lean and are load-bearing for the EnrichedCategory Cat instance.
CFund now has an enriched category structure

PROVED in Braid/Purity.lean (green, sorry-free): permHom_braidPhi_eq_one_of_concProjN' — a loop of executions whose chain-zigzag is trivial has a braid with trivial permutation, i.e. it is PURE. Plus the split exactness (stdSectionN_comp_chainProjN' = rfl; stdSectionGrpdN_comp_concProjN' = 𝟭; loopProj_surjective). Right square commutes via braidPhi_comp_permHom + concRho_forget'.

Flow/Fund.lean landed, green, sorry-free. def Fund + EnrichedCategory Cat (Fund K); Bicategory.Strict (CatEnriched (Fund K)) derives by inferInstance. Hom u v = FreeGroupoid ((Ch (K.repoint u v))ᵒᵖ) — the OPPOSITE, so it is exactly what CFund's concProj lands in. comp = fundConc via lift₂; the six laws are chConcatOp_obj_* / _map_*, which are just chConc_assoc / chConc_id_left / chConc_id_right and their morphism counterparts from Flow/ChainConcat. Built first try from the CFund template: everything hard in CFund lived in the LINES (linesRestrict_chConcMor); Fund has none.

Flow/Project.lean landed, green, sorry-free. cfundToFund K : EnrichedFunctor Cat (CFund K) (Fund K) — identity on vertices, flowProj = FreeGroupoid.map (CategoryOfElements.π (Lines ...)) on hom-objects. map_comp IS the content (concGrpdConc_comp_flowProj: forgetting the line commutes with concatenation), and on generators it is concConc_comp_π := rfl — because the chain half of concConc IS chConc. lift₂_ext carries it to the groupoids.

BRAID_ENRICHMENT.md §1 and §4 rewritten. Killed both false claims: (a) 'CFund = pi_1(K) with every hom-set replaced by a BRAID GROUP' — hom-objects are GROUPOIDS not groups, and their vertex groups are PURE and REDUCIBLE (P_n for a cube, prod_i P_{d_i} for a wedge); the full braid group is never a cube's vertex group. (b) 'CFund is Grpd-enriched' — it is Cat-enriched (mathlib gives Grpd no monoidal structure). §4 now carries the DEGENERACY THEOREM for B-enrichment (unit forces l(x,x)=0, composition forces additivity, z:=x and l>=0 in N give l==0, so a B-enriched category is just a set) and the correct positive statement: CFund is Cat-enriched (hence Bicategory.Strict via CatEnriched) and carries the braid grading braidGrading : Int(Lines K) => B. Also flagged that BraidDeloop cannot be the tensor target (Localization.uniq), and added the purity result to §1's invariants — plus an explicit warning not to say 'ker Phi is the branching ambiguity' (the proved statement is about ker of the PROJECTION, and Phi does not factor through it).

Salvetti/BRAID.md rewritten: 200 lines -> 121, retitled 'The braiding is created, not inherited'. DELETED the sections describing G K := ⨆ₙ ConcGrpd (K^⊗n) ⋊ Sₙ (§0, §3, most of §5, and the §7 status table) — none of it exists in the repo, and the status table was also wrong (it marked the Day tensor 'in progress'; it is done). SALVAGED and kept, all verified against the code: §1 the Yoneda proof that (PrecubicalSet, ⊗) has NO swap (the whole reason the braiding is CREATED); §2 ConcGrpd + the local model concWedgeSerialEquiv (checked: it IS proved, FreeGroupoidProd.lean:102) with a new warning that it routes through Localization.uniq and so is useless for strictness; §3 salCross/salWind + β²≠𝟙 for □² + the Mazurkiewicz slogan, now also naming salCrossVec and why writhe is strictly coarser than the pure-braid data; §4 the E₂ argument + the machine-verified Betti numbers. Added §5 as an explicit DO-NOT-RESURRECT for G K, retaining its two genuinely useful warnings (ConcGrpd is not monoidal; the composition-to-tensor statement is about the DELOOPING, and BraidDeloop cannot serve it). Also added the Z-vs-□ⁿ contrast to §1: unlabelled events give two PARALLEL refinement arrows, which IS the braid generator; labelling is what turns Bₙ into Pₙ.

MECHANISM DECIDED AND APPLIED: a second lean_lib (CubeChainsBraid), not a subdirectory move and not the .ignore treatment. lakefile.toml gains [[lean_lib]] name = 'CubeChainsBraid'; the root CubeChainsBraid.lean imports the braid result's endpoints, so the target builds exactly its import cone. 'lake build CubeChainsBraid' is green. Documented in ONE place: ARCHITECTURE.md 'Build & conventions'. Nothing was moved or deleted — Testing/'s counterexamples (trinity, TwoSquares, EventNamingCounterexample) stay exactly where they are, just outside the braid target, as this ticket required.

*** THE AUDIT NOTE ON THIS TICKET WAS STALE. *** It said 'only 5 of 16 Schedule/ files are quarantinable; the other 11 — including Space, Cover, Cone, ChainCone, Atlas, HDA, LabelSpace — are INSIDE the braid cone'. Re-measured: the cone is 68 of the 122 non-Testing modules and contains only TWO Schedule/ files, HDA and Orientation (the latter is w1). NOT Space, NOT Cover, NOT Cone, NOT ChainCone, NOT Atlas, NOT LabelSpace. Zero Cylinder/, zero Cobordisms/. The Events/ extraction (Cubical-7sa) plus the move of the braid thread into Braid/ is what changed it.

All three acceptance criteria met. (1) lake build CubeChainsBraid is GREEN. (2) The 68-module cone is sorry-free — verified by Lean's own 'declaration uses sorry' warning (none), not by grep (grep gives a false positive on the word 'admit' in a SegalAltitude docstring heading). (3) #print axioms reports exactly [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound] for hasGlobalEventNaming_iff_braidPure, permHom_braidPhi_eq_one_of_concProjN', braidGrpd, and cfundToFund. The gate lives IN the target root, so it re-runs on every build.

All 4 children complete. The braid thread is isolated by a second lean_lib: 'lake build CubeChainsBraid' is green, sorry-free, and axiom-clean ([propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]), building exactly the 68-module import cone of the braid result — no Cylinder/, no Cobordisms/, no Testing/, and only 2 of 16 Schedule/ files (HDA, Orientation=w1). Mechanism documented in ARCHITECTURE.md 'Build & conventions'. Nothing moved or deleted.

WON'T BUILD — user decision (2026-07-14). The delooping and the CFund→B(Braid) 2-functor are being dropped. Conceptual reason: an EnrichedFunctor to the delooping IS just 'braidGrpd on each hom-object, compatibly with composition' — the delooping adds no content, only packaging, and its one-object target collapses the 1-cell structure. The only real statement (composition ↦ braid tensor) is a single functor equation braidGrpd(concat) = braidsTensor(braidGrpd, braidGrpd), stateable without the enriched instance — but the user is deferring even that. The whole braid-tensor cluster (Salvetti/BraidDeloop, Braid/Deloop, Braid/JuxAssoc, Braid/Jux, Braid/BlockPerm, braidsTensor) is being removed as dead. juxHom_assoc, blockPerm, etc. are ~20-line rebuilds if ever wanted.

WON'T BUILD — user decision (2026-07-14), same as ndf.3. The monoidal 2-functor CFund → B(Braid) is dropped. If ever wanted, it is NOT a separate construction: it is braidGrpd assembled across hom-objects, and its only content is the equation braidGrpd(concGrpdConc x y) = braidsTensor(braidGrpd x, braidGrpd y), which reduces on generators to 'the event permutation of a concatenation is the block permutation of the two'. No delooping-as-enriched-category needed.

Finite substrate landed: FinPre (dim+face table) → toConcrete (reuses PrecubicalConstructions face/vertex API) → toBPSet (bipointed Nerve). coface/faceMap/cubeMap/initVertexMap/finalVertexMap/vertex₀/₁ were only defensively noncomputable (cube-Yoneda canonicalMap is genuinely computable), so markers removed — no csimp needed. #eval of interval/square faces (v1, e_1) and extremal vertices (v0, c00, c11) run end-to-end; face path also machine-checked via readCell_faceMap_mkCell. Axioms clean (no sorryAx/native_decide).

Wedge pushout made computable. NOT via csimp (impossible: mathlib pushout object is Classical.choice-opaque, so no provable = to a computable object). Instead: new Foundations/GluePushout.lean assembles the pushout pointwise from the explicit Types.Pushout (a Quot) via combineCocones; Glue.isPushout gives the universal property; core defs are @[irreducible] so isDefEq stops at the boundary (as at Classical.choice in mathlib's pushout) while the compiler still #evals. wedge2.toPsh := Glue.gluePsh (in place); ~150 pushout.* -> Glue.* references reframed across WedgeMap/Segal/SegalSplit/SegalAltitude/Correspondence/Flow/ChainConcat/LinesWedge/Reversal (all IsPushout-based, transfer verbatim). Delivered in 3 committed phases (A: drop defensive markers; B: pushout swap; C: fixpoint -> computable up to nEvents). Acceptance met: Testing/WedgeCompute.lean #evals a wedge[2,2] cell (0,[none,none]) and a concrete wedge-map (ιᵂ) action (1,[none,none]) on the REAL serialWedge; native_decide anchor; whole build green, sorry-free.

Events/lines leaf layer is computable: blockIdx/blockFace genuine (no .choose), consumers un-noncomputable, chamber order decidable, nEvents = dims-sum. Build green except collaborator's Surjectivity WIP.

evPerm computes: keyEquiv/eventEquiv given explicit Fintype.bijInv inverses, evIdx/evIdx'/evPerm/evPerm'/fineBead/keyRank de-noncomputable, eventObjDecidableEq added. #eval evPerm' on a concrete ConcCat execution yields the identity perm (native_decide passes); full build green except collaborator's Surjectivity WIP. Non-trivial two-execution braiding witness is gvp.7.

permWord emitter done: wordToBraid (permWord σ) = ofPerm σ and length = permLen, both proven; permWordZ signed GAP form; braidWord f := permWord (evPerm' f) tied to braidGrading's braid content. Full build green. #eval: swap(0,2)/Fin3 → word [0,1,0], signed [1,2,1], length 3. Nonempty-word-from-a-real-reordering-refinement is gvp.7.

End-to-end DONE: refinements are computable (surjInv -> finite preimEvent search; ~20 spurious noncomputable markers removed across ChainPartition/SalBraidChain/Normalize/ElementaryBraiding). braidGrading #evals on a real cube refinement (Testing/CubeRefineBraid.lean: braidWordZ (seqMor cube3exec revLine) = [1,2,1]), faithful via braidGrading_map_braidWord. THE HARNESS is live: define K + execution + reordering -> braid word. Also demonstrated on the terminal precubical set -> B4 (Testing/TerminalBraids.lean). Axiom gate reinterpreted: Classical.choice is mathlib-Finset-deep, never runtime; the meaningful gate (no sorryAx) holds. Full build green (2112).
Zig-zag -> concatenated signed word done: wordZToBraid/invWordZ + loopWord, sanity native_decide'd on the terminal set. Signed concatenation, no normalization (as scoped).

All actionable children (gvp.2-8) closed. gvp.1 remains a PINNED design-record reference. Computable braidGrading tower + Artin-word emitter + loop layer + computable refinements landed; harness (define K -> braid words) live, demonstrated on cubes (P3) and the terminal set (B4).
